## שלח

_Section: שלח_

**Hebrew:**

## 271

### שלח

אין ענטפער אויף דער ידיעה וועגן אייער פאָרשטייענדן יערלעכן לאָנטשאָן ביום א' פרשת שלח לך, הבע"ל, יהי רצון אַז די אונטערנעמונג זאָל זיין מיט גרויס הצלחה אין אַלע הינזיכטן.
עס איז באַקאַנט דער וואָרט פון אַלטן רבי'ן, בעל התניא והשלחן ערוך, אַז די פרשה פון דער וואָך אַנטהאַלט אָנווייזונגען פאַר דעם טאָג­טעגלעכן לעבן, ספּעציעל אין צוזאַמענהאַנג מיט אויסערגעוויינלעכע געשעענישן.
די אונטערנעמונג היינטיקס יאָר קומט פאָר אין דער וואָך פון דער פרשה שלח[ftnref\_1810\_\*], וואו עס ווערט דערציילט, אַז משה רבנו האָט געשיקט שלוחים אַרומקוקן ארץ­ישראל, און אַז די שלוחים האָבן געבראַכט אַ ניט­ריכטיקן באַריכט. דערציילן אונזערע חכמים, זכרונם לברכה, אַז די פרויען האָבן זיך ניט אָפּגעשראָקן פאַר דעם אומגינסטיקן באַריכט און זיי האָבן ווייטער געוועלט אָנהאַלטן זייער וועג צו ארץ ישראל, ארץ הקודש, לויט דעם אויבערשטנס רצון. די פרויען זיינען געווען דורכגעדרונגען מיט דעם געפיל, אַז ארץ ישראל איז אַ לאַנד פון קדושה און ספּעציעלע ג­טלעכע השגחה, ווי די תורה זאָגט: ארץ אשר עיני ה' אלקיך בה מראשית השנה ועד אחרית שנה — אַ לאַנד וואו די ספּעציעלע ג­טלעכע השגחה איז פון אָנהויבס יאָר ביז ענדע יאָר.
ווי עס איז מערערע מאָל אונטערשטראָכן געוואָרן, איז די תורה אַן אייביקע, און אַלע אירע אָנווייזונגען זיינען אייביקע, דאָס הייסט גילטיק אומעטום און אין אַלע צייטן.

## 272

די צוגעבונדנקייט פון דער אידישער פרוי צו "ארץ הקדש" איז אַן אויסדרוק פון איר איבערגעבנקייט צו אַלע ענינים פון קדושה, תורה ומצוות, וואָס דריקט זיך אויס נאָך אין אַ שטאַרקערע פאָרם ווי באַ די מענער, פון צייט צו צייט.
דערפון איז אַן אָנווייזונג אויך פאַר אונזער דור, אַז די פרויען קענען פיל אויפטאָן צו פאַרשטאַרקן אַלע ענינים פון קדושה, תורה ומצוות, צוערשט אין זייער אייגענע היים און צוזאַמען דערמיט אויך אין דער סביבה אין אַ פּאַסנדן אופן, און אַזוי אַרום מאַכן באַ זיך אינדערהיים און אין דער סביבה אַן "ארץ הקדש" פאַר דער ג­טלעכער שכינה.
דאָס איז דאָך פון די אויפגאַבען פון די חב"ד פרויען אומעטום, וועלכע האָבן נאָך דעם ספּעציעלן זכות וואָס זייער וועג איז באַלויכטן מיט דער ליכטיקייט פון חסידות און חסידישע טראַדיציעס.
און פּונקט ווי צוזאַמען מיט ארץ ישראל זיינען געגעבן געוואָרן אַלע ג­טלעכע ברכות פון ארץ זבת חלב ודבש אויך אין דעם מאַטעריעלן זין, אויב נאָר מען וועט זיך אויפפירן לויט דעם אויבערשטנס רצון, אַזוי זיינען שטענדיק אָנגעגרייט אַלע
ג­טלעכע ברכות פאַר אַלעמען וועלכע העלפן פאַרשטאַרקן און פאַרשפּרייטן תורה­אידישקייט, לויט זייערע פולסטע מעגלעכקייטן.
השם יתברך זאָל מצליח זיין אייער אַרבעט אין אַ שטייגענדער מאָס, און זאָל אייך באַגליקן בטוב הנראה והנגלה אין אַלץ וואָס מען נויטיקט זיך, בגשמיות וברוחניות גם יחד.
בברכה לבשורות טובות.
[quotation](ממכתב י"ג סיון, תשכ"ה)[/quotation]
[new\_section]
... לכתבו אשר ישנם מלעיגים עליו — לא דבר חדש הוא שמלעיגים על התומ"צ. וכן הי' חלקם של כו"כ החרדים לדבר ה', מתחיל מאברהם אבינו. וראה התחלת (ויסוד) כל ד"ח שו"ע: ולא יתבייש כו'. וכבר נאמר — ה' אתנו אל תיראום.
[quotation](ממכתב כ"ג מ"ח, תשכ"ג)[/quotation]

**English:**

## 271

### Shelach

I acknowledge receipt of the information regarding your upcoming annual luncheon on Sunday, Parshas Shelach, may it be successful in every respect.

It is a well-known teaching of the Alter Rebbe, the Baal HaTanya and Shulchan Aruch, that the weekly Parsha contains guidance for daily life, especially in connection with extraordinary events.

This year’s event takes place during the week of Parshas Shelach, which recounts how Moshe Rabbeinu sent scouts to survey Eretz Yisrael, and how those scouts brought back a negative report. Our Sages, of blessed memory, tell us that the women were not deterred by this unfavorable report; they continued to desire to reach Eretz Yisrael, Eretz HaKodesh, in accordance with the will of the Ribono shel Olam. The women were imbued with the feeling that Eretz Yisrael is a land of holiness and special Divine providence, as the Torah states: "A land which Hashem your G-d cares for; the eyes of Hashem your G-d are always upon it, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year."

As has been emphasized many times, the Torah is eternal, and all its instructions are eternal—meaning they are valid everywhere and at all times.

## 272

The attachment of the Jewish woman to "Eretz HaKodesh" is an expression of her dedication to all matters of holiness, Torah, and mitzvos, which manifests in an even stronger form than that of men, from time to time.

From this, there is also guidance for our generation: that women can accomplish much to strengthen all matters of holiness, Torah, and mitzvos—first and foremost in their own homes, and together with that, in their surroundings in an appropriate manner—and in this way, create within their homes and their surroundings an "Eretz HaKodesh" for the Divine Shechinah.

This is, after all, one of the tasks of Chabad women everywhere, who possess the additional special merit that their path is illuminated by the light of Chassidus and Chassidic traditions.

And just as, together with Eretz Yisrael, all the Divine blessings of a "land flowing with milk and honey" were given—also in the material sense—provided that one conducts oneself according to the will of the Ribono shel Olam, so too are all Divine blessings always prepared for everyone who helps to strengthen and spread Torah-Yiddishkeit to the fullest extent of their capabilities.

May Hashem Yisbarach grant success to your work in an ever-increasing measure, and may He bless you with revealed and manifest good in everything you need, in both gashmius and ruchnius alike.

With blessings for good tidings.
(From a letter dated 13 Sivan, 5725)

...Regarding what you wrote, that there are those who mock him—it is nothing new that people mock those who observe Torah and mitzvos. This was the lot of many who were zealous for the word of Hashem, beginning with Avraham Avinu. See the beginning (and foundation) of all of Shulchan Aruch: "And he should not be ashamed..." And it has already been said: "Hashem is with us; do not fear them."
(From a letter dated 23 MarCheshvan, 5723)

---


## כח סיון

_Section: כח סיון_

**Hebrew:**

## 275

דמוסדות אלו במשך עשר שנותיו האחרונות דכ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר בחיים חיותו בעלמא דין, ומוסיף והולך ואור גם לאחרי הסתלקותו, ובאופן ד"מעלין בקודש"[ftnref\_1817\_6].
ונקודת ההסברה בזה — ע"פ הכלל הידוע "ירידה צורך עלי'": כיון שהביאה לחצי כדור התחתון, "גלות אמריקא"[ftnref\_1818\_7], היוותה ירידה גדולה ביותר [נוסף על הירידה שבשאר הגליות ש"גלתה[ftnref\_1819\_8] ליובאַוויטש"[ftnref\_1820\_9]], הרי מובן, שהכוונה והתכלית בזה היא — העלי' הבאה עי"ז, עד לעלי' שבאין­ערוך (בהתאם להירידה שבאין­ערוך), שזוהי ההוספה והחידוש שנעשה בכללות העבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" דוקא בחצי כדור התחתון, באופן נעלה יותר, ועד — שלא בערך.
ועדיין צריך ביאור בשייכות ענין זה לתוכן העבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" — ה"סיסמה" של תורת החסידות בכלל[ftnref\_1814\_3] וחסידות חב"ד בפרט — דמכיון שכל ענין הוא בהשגחה פרטית[ftnref\_1822\_10], והקב"ה סיבב את הסיבות שהמרכז של חסידות חב"ד — כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר וחסידיו — הועבר והושתל בחצי כדור התחתון, צריך לומר, שזה נוגע במיוחד לשלימות הענין ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה".
ב. ויובן זה בהקדים הביאור בדיוק הלשון (והתוכן) "יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה":
מכיון שלשון זה הוא פסוק בתורה שבכתב, אשר כל תיבה ותיבה שבה היא בתכלית הדיוק, מובן, שבהעבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" יש הדגשה (והוראה) מיוחדת בכל אחת מג' תיבות אלו (נוסף על התוכן הכללי שבכולן יחד)[ftnref\_1823\_11].
ועוד זאת: כל ביטוי ב"תורת אמת" — מכוון לתוכנו האמיתי של הביטוי, כפי שהוא בתכלית השלימות, ועד באופן בלתי מוגבל. ומזה מובן גם בנוגע לעניננו, שהכוונה בכל אחת מג' תיבות הנ"ל — "יפוצו", "מעינותיך", ו"חוצה" — היא למלוא מובן המלה, עד באופן בלתי מוגבל.
ויש לומר, שעד"ז הוא בנוגע לההוספה ב"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" שע"י (גאולת י"ב תמוז וע"י) ההעברה לחצי כדור התחתון — (א) הוספה בכל פרט מג' ענינים אלה; (ב) וההוספה בכל א' מהם היא הוספה אמיתית באופן בלתי מוגבל (כדלקמן ס"ז).
ג. ביאור הדברים:

## 277

להענינים כפי שהם בשרשם ומקורם, עצם התורה — "סתים דאורייתא" (פנימיות התורה) שמתקשרת עם "סתים דקוב"ה"[ftnref\_1833\_21], עד לעצמותו ומהותו ית'.
ונוסף לזה י"ל, ש"מעינותיך" קאי על האדם המפיץ — שהתעסקותו בהעבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" צ"ל לא רק בכחות הגלויים שלו (כפי שנמשכו כבר משרשם ומקורם), שאז התעסקותו בזה היא במדידה והגבלה, אלא גם בכחות הנעלמים שלו כפי שהם כלולים בשרשם ומקורם בעצם הנפש ("מעינותיך"), שאז התעסקותו בזה היא באופן שלמעלה ממדידה והגבלה.
ה. "חוצה":
בכללות מציאות העולם יש שני חלקים: "פנים" ו"חוץ". ובכל אחד מהם כו"כ דרגות: מפנים שבפנים עד לחוץ שבחוץ, עד ל"חוצה" שאין חוצה ממנו. ומזה מובן גם בנוגע ל"חוצה" בנדו"ד, שיש בו כמה דרגות: לגבי בחינת "מעינותיך", גם הענינים דקדושה (אפילו דרגות נעלות בקדושה, אבל למטה מבחי' "מעינותיך") נקראים בשם "חוצה"; ויש "חוצה" כפשוטו — ענינים שהם מחוץ לגבול הקדושה, ובהם עצמם יש ג"כ כו"כ דרגות, עד ל"חוצה" שאין חוצה ממנו.
ו"חוצה" בלשון התורה (תורת אמת) פירושו — "חוצה" בלי כל הגבלות[ftnref\_1834\_22], הכולל גם "חוצה" שאין חוצה ממנו (ע"ד "למטה מטה עד אין תכלית"[ftnref\_1832\_20]); והיינו, שהעבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך" צריכה להיות בכל הדרגות ד"חוצה", עד ב"חוצה" שאין חוצה ממנו.
ו. ויש לומר, שג' ענינים אלה תלויים זה בזה:
כאשר התעסקות האדם בלימוד והפצת תורת החסידות היא ב"עצם" הנפש שלו, שלמעלה ממדידה והגבלה, הרי גם התורה שמלמד ומפיץ — היא בלי הגבלות, עד שלא נשאר אצלו שום ענין (גם הענינים הכי נעלים בהתורה, עד ל"עצם" התורה) שאינו מתפשט לחוץ; וגילוי עצם הנפש (ועי"ז — עצם התורה) נעשה ע"י החוצה — כמבואר בהמשך ההילולא[ftnref\_1836\_23], שבכדי לנצח את המלחמה ("שהנצחון נטוע ומושרש בעצם הנפש למעלה יותר מכחות הגלויים שבנפש"), "מבזבז (המלך) כל סגולות האוצרות דהון יקר הנאסף והנקבץ במשך כמה שנים מדור אחר דור, ואשר מעולם

## 279

באמיתתו ובשלימותו, כלומר, כל מה שנכלל ב"שלח". כי, באם ישנו פרט מסויים שנכלל במשמעותו של "שלח", ועדיין לא נעשה בפועל — אין זה "שלח" לאמיתתו ובשלימותו.
ויש לומר, ש(בכללות) יש בזה ג' ענינים — בהתאם להג' ענינים ד"יפוצו", "מעינותיך", ו"חוצה":
(א) אופן פעולת השליחות — בפיזור ללא כל הגבלות, שכן, כל זמן שפעולת השליחות יכולה להיות בפיזור גדול יותר, אי אפשר לומר, שאופן הפעולה בזה היא לאמיתתה; ובסגנון האמור — אמיתית הענין ד"יפוצו".
(ב) הדבר (האדם ששולח, והתורה) הנשלח ממנו — לא רק דבר חיצוני או בינוני כו', אלא דבר הכי נעלה, עד לשרש ומקור ועצם הדבר, שכן, כל זמן שיש דרגא נעלית יותר (בו בעצמו, ובהתורה) שלא שלח, עדיין אין זה "שלח" לאמיתתו; ובסגנון האמור — אמיתית הענין ד"מעינותיך".
(ג) המקום שאליו שולחים — לא רק בקירוב מקום, אלא גם בריחוק מקום, ולא רק ריחוק מקום ביחס אליו, אלא מקום הכי רחוק שאין רחוק יותר ממנו, שכן, כל זמן שישנו מקום רחוק יותר, הרי עדיין לא נעשתה השליחות מחוץ הימנו לאמיתתו; ובסגנון האמור — אמיתית הענין ד"חוצה".
ט. וע"פ דברי כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר במכתבו[ftnref\_1840\_27] לחגיגת י"ב תמוז הראשונה[ftnref\_1841\_28] — "לא אותי בלבד גאל הקב"ה בי"ב תמוז, כי אם גם את כל מחבבי תורתנו הק', שומרי מצוה, וגם את אשר בשם ישראל יכונה", מובן, שכל האמור לעיל בנוגע להעילוי שנפעל בהעבודה ד"יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" ע"י ההעברה לחצי כדור התחתון — שבאה כתוצאה מגאולת י"ב­י"ג תמוז — שייך לכל אחד ואחת מישראל, גם לזה "אשר בשם ישראל יכונה"[ftnref\_1842\_29].
ובפשטות — שאצל כאו"א צ"ל הענין והעבודה ד"שלח", "יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה" — בכחו של נשיא הדור, שהטיל שליחות ("שלח") על כאו"א לעסוק בהפצת המעינות חוצה.
ובפרטיות יותר — בכל ג' ענינים האמורים: "יפוצו" — שהלימוד מגיע וחודר בכל חלקי המקום שבו מלמד תורה זו. וגם בנוגע להתורה שמפיץ — לא יחשוב,

**English:**

## 275

These institutions [were established] during the final ten years of the Rebbe’s life in this world, and they continue to grow in light even after his passing, in a manner of "ascending in holiness".

The core explanation for this—based on the well-known principle that "a descent is for the sake of an ascent"—is as follows: Since the move to the lower hemisphere, the "American exile", constituted an extremely great descent [in addition to the descent of the other exiles, where "Lubavitch went into exile"], it is understood that the intent and purpose behind it is the subsequent ascent, reaching an incomparable ascent (corresponding to the incomparable descent). This is the addition and innovation achieved in the general work of "let your springs spread outward" specifically in the lower hemisphere, in a more elevated manner, reaching even to the level of the incomparable.

Yet, there still remains a need to explain the connection of this matter to the content of the work of "let your springs spread outward"—the "slogan" of the teachings of Chassidus in general, and Chabad Chassidus in particular. Since every matter is by Divine Providence, and Hashem orchestrated the circumstances so that the center of Chabad Chassidus—the Rebbe and his Chassidim—was transferred and planted in the lower hemisphere, it must be said that this relates specifically to the perfection of the matter of "let your springs spread outward."

b. This can be understood by first explaining the precision of the language (and content) of "let your springs spread outward" (*yifu tzu mayanosecha chutzah*):

Since this phrasing is a verse in the Written Torah, where every single word is of the utmost precision, it is understood that in the work of "let your springs spread outward" there is a special emphasis (and instruction) in each of these three words (in addition to the general content contained in all of them together).

Furthermore: Every expression in the "Torah of Truth" is directed toward the true content of the expression, as it exists in the utmost perfection, and even in an unlimited manner. From this, it is also understood regarding our subject, that the intent in each of these three words—"spread" (*yifu tzu*), "your springs" (*mayanosecha*), and "outward" (*chutzah*)—is to the full meaning of the word, even in an unlimited manner.

It can be said that the same applies to the addition in "let your springs spread outward" that comes about through (the redemption of 12 Tammuz and through) the transfer to the lower hemisphere: (a) an addition in every detail of these three matters; (b) and the addition in each one of them is a true addition in an unlimited manner (as will be explained in section 7).

c. Explanation of the matter:

## 277

To the matters as they are in their root and source, the essence of Torah—the "hidden of the Torah" (the inner dimension of the Torah) which connects with the "hidden of Hashem", reaching up to His very Essence and Being.

In addition to this, it can be said that "your springs" refers to the person who is spreading—that his engagement in the work of "let your springs spread outward" must be not only with his revealed faculties (as they have already been drawn down from their root and source), for then his engagement in this is with measurement and limitation, but also with his hidden faculties as they are included in their root and source in the essence of the soul ("your springs"), for then his engagement in this is in a manner that is above measurement and limitation.

e. "Outward" (*chutzah*):

In the general reality of the world, there are two parts: "inside" (*panim*) and "outside" (*chutz*). And in each of them there are many levels: from the innermost of the inside to the outermost of the outside, until the "outward" beyond which there is no further outward. From this, it is also understood regarding the "outward" in our case, that it has several levels: relative to the level of "your springs," even matters of holiness (even high levels of holiness, but lower than the level of "your springs") are called "outward"; and there is "outward" in its literal sense—matters that are outside the boundary of holiness, and within them themselves there are also many levels, until the "outward" beyond which there is no further outward.

And "outward" in the language of the Torah (the Torah of Truth) means—"outward" without any limitations, which includes also the "outward" beyond which there is no further outward (similar to "down, down, until there is no end"); meaning, that the work of "let your springs spread" must be in all the levels of "outward," even in the "outward" beyond which there is no further outward.

f. It can be said that these three matters are dependent upon one another:

When a person's engagement in studying and spreading the teachings of Chassidus is from the "essence" of his soul, which is above measurement and limitation, then the Torah that he teaches and spreads is also without limitations, until there remains no matter for him (even the most elevated matters in the Torah, up to the "essence" of the Torah) that does not spread outward; and the revelation of the essence of the soul (and through this—the essence of the Torah) is brought about by the "outward"—as explained in the continuation of the *hillula*, that in order to win the war ("for the victory is planted and rooted in the essence of the soul, higher than the revealed faculties in the soul"), "(the King) spends all the treasures of the precious wealth collected and gathered over many years from generation to generation, and which from time immemorial

## 279

In its truth and perfection, meaning, everything that is included in "send" (*shelach*). For, if there is a specific detail that is included in the meaning of "send," and it has not yet been done in practice—this is not "send" in its truth and perfection.

It can be said that (in general) there are three matters in this—corresponding to the three matters of "spread," "your springs," and "outward":

(a) The manner of the action of the mission—in spreading without any limitations, for as long as the action of the mission can be in greater spreading, it cannot be said that the manner of the action in this is in its truth; and in the terminology mentioned above—the truth of the matter of "spread."

(b) The thing (the person who sends, and the Torah) that is sent from him—not just an external or intermediate thing, etc., but the most elevated thing, up to the root and source and essence of the thing, for as long as there is a more elevated level (in it itself, and in the Torah) that he has not sent, it is still not "send" in its truth; and in the terminology mentioned above—the truth of the matter of "your springs."

(c) The place to which one sends—not only in proximity of place, but also in distance of place, and not only distance of place relative to him, but the most distant place than which there is none more distant, for as long as there is a more distant place, then the mission "outward" has not yet been done in its truth; and in the terminology mentioned above—the truth of the matter of "outward."

ix. And according to the words of the Rebbe in his letter for the first celebration of 12 Tammuz —"Hashem did not redeem only me on 12 Tammuz, but also all those who love our holy Torah, the observers of mitzvos, and also those who are called by the name of Israel," it is understood that everything stated above regarding the elevation achieved in the work of "let your springs spread outward" through the transfer to the lower hemisphere—which came as a result of the redemption of 12-13 Tammuz—belongs to every single Jew, even to the one "who is called by the name of Israel".

And simply—that for each and every one there must be the matter and the work of "send," "let your springs spread outward"—through the power of the Nasi of the generation, who placed a mission ("send") upon each and every one to engage in spreading the springs outward.

And more specifically—in all three matters mentioned: "spread"—that the study reaches and penetrates into all parts of the place where one teaches this Torah. And also regarding the Torah that one spreads—one should not think,

_Section: קרח_

**Hebrew:**

## 281

### קרח

... ת"ח ת"ח על השתדלותו הרבה בקשר להחזית דתית טכנית[ftnref\_1846\_\*] ועל שלא נמנע אפילו מללכת לפ' וכו'. והרי בכיו"ב אפילו על ספק וס"ס כדאית העשי', ולהעיר מסנה' (קי, א) ובפרט לדעת הרמב"ן (על שרש השמיני בסהמ"צ להרמב"ם) שעפ"ז ויקם משה וילך גו'. . .
[quotation](ממכתב אלול, תשכ"ה)[/quotation]

**English:**

## 281

### Korach

... *Tashar, tashar* [a term of praise/encouragement] for your great efforts regarding the technical religious front, and for the fact that you did not refrain even from going to [the authorities/the relevant office], etc. And surely, in a case such as this, even for a doubt or a double doubt, one should act, as evidenced by *Sanhedrin* (110a), and especially according to the opinion of the Ramban (in his commentary on the Eighth Principle of the Rambam’s *Sefer HaMitzvos*), according to which [the verse] "And Moshe arose and went," etc....
[quotation](From a letter, Elul 5725)[/quotation]

## חוקת

_Section: חוקת_

**Hebrew:**

## 281

### חוקת

בהמשך לשיחתנו פעמים ע"ד השתדלותו בדבר אמצעים הכספים להדפסת התניא.
בודאי יודיענו תוצאות פעולתו בזה, ולמותר להאריך בגודל נחיצות הענין ורום ערכו, ותקותי חזקה אשר יעשה ככל יכולתו בזה, ומגלגלים זכות —
זיכוך אויר העולם ע"י אותיות התורה והתפלה, תולע ותולעת דמעשה הפרה וכמש"פ בלקו"ת ד"ה זאת חקת ס"ד.
וי"ל דבלמוד התניא שזהו לקוטי עצות בעבודה שבלב ישנם שני הענינים —
ע"י זכאי (ולהעיר מחילוק כהנים ולוים אשר לוים[ftnref\_1847\_\*] הם בחי' טהור).
[quotation](ממכתב עש"ק פ' פרה תשג"ה) [/quotation]

**English:**

## 281

### Chukas

Following up on our previous conversations regarding your efforts to secure the necessary funds for printing the Tanya.

You will surely inform us of the results of your efforts in this matter. It is unnecessary to elaborate on the great necessity of this project and its lofty value, and I have a strong hope that you will do everything in your power regarding this, for "merit is brought about through those who are meritorious"—

The refinement of the atmosphere of the world through the letters of Torah and tefillah, the *tola* and *tola'as* of the *parah* (red heifer) ritual, as explained in *Likutei Torah* in the *maamar* beginning "Zos Chukas" (section 4).

And it may be said that in the study of Tanya—which is a collection of advice for the *avodah* of the heart—there are two aspects:

Through one who is *zakai* (meritorious)—and it is worth noting the distinction between Kohanim and Levi'im, where the Levi'im are the level of *tahor* (pure).

(From a letter, Erev Shabbos Parshas Parah, 5705)

## פינחס

_Section: פינחס_

**Hebrew:**

## 296

### פינחס

... ומה שהעיר מלקו"ת ס"פ פנחס ד"ה קדש ישראל רפ"ד שכתב שם איזה פעמים קדש האדם, ותמה אצ"ל ישראל.
בטח דפוס ישן אצלו, וכבר נתקן בדפוסים שאח"כ. וכנראה מפני יראת הצענזאר הדפיסו מתחילה האדם.
וראה אור התורה להצ"צ שמות ע' פ"ו ובהערות שם.
והשי"ת יהפך ימים אלו לששון ולשמחה ויתגלה לעיני כל בשר אשר קדש ישראל לה' גו' כל אוכליו יאשמו בעגלא דידן.
[quotation](ממכתב ו' מנ"א, השי"ת)[/quotation]

**English:**

## 296

### Pinchas

Regarding the point raised from *Likutei Torah*, end of *Parshas Pinchas*, *dih* "Kodesh Yisrael," page 284, where the text appears several times as "Kodesh HaAdam" (the person is holy), and you expressed surprise, as it should surely be "Yisrael" (Israel is holy):

You must be using an old edition; this has already been corrected in subsequent printings. It appears that due to fear of the censor, it was initially printed as "HaAdam."

See *Ohr HaTorah* by the Tzemach Tzedek, *Shemos*, page 86, and the notes there.

May the Ribono shel Olam turn these days into gladness and joy, and may it be revealed to the eyes of all flesh that "Israel is holy to Hashem... all who devour him shall be held guilty," speedily in our days.

(From a letter dated 6 Menachem Av, the Ribono shel Olam)

## מטות

_Section: מטות_

**Hebrew:**

## 296

### מטות

... קבלתי מכתבו עם הידיעה שמשנה דירתו למקום חדש, ונתקבל קודם נסעי על ציון כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר, וכבקשתו הזכרתיו על הציון, שיהי' משנה מקום משנה מזל לטובה בגשמיות וברוחניות גם יחד, אשר חירות אמתית היא כששניהם הולכים יחדו, שלום בפמליא של מטה, שעי"ז נעשה ג"כ שלום בפמליא של מעלה (יעוין לקו"ת ס"פ מטות), ונמשך ברכה והצלחה כיון שהכלי — שלום. . .
[quotation](ממכתב ז' ניסן, תשי"ג)[/quotation]

**English:**

## 296

### Matos

...I have received your letter with the news that you are moving to a new location. It arrived before my visit to the resting place of my father-in-law, the Rebbe, and as you requested, I mentioned you at the tziyun, that your change of place should be a change of fortune for the good, in both material and spiritual matters alike. For true freedom is when both go together—peace in the assembly below, which thereby brings about peace in the assembly above (see Likutei Torah, end of Parshas Matos), and draws down blessing and success, since the vessel [for blessing] is peace...
[quotation](From a letter dated 7 Nissan, 5713)[/quotation]

---


## בין המצרים

_Section: בין המצרים_

**Hebrew:**

## 297

### בין המצרים

ואוקמי אחזקה — לדבר ושקו"ט שם בהל' שהזמ"ג הל' ביהב"ח (ואו"ת דפ' פנחס).
וה"ז דין ומנהג העיקרי בביהמ"צ, לא רק לבטלם כ"א להפכם לששון ושמחה ומועדים טובים (והאמת והשלום אהבו — כמש"כ גם בהקדמתו לספרו[ftnref\_1854\_\*] שנת' בת"ח, מצו"ב ח"י דולר)
ואשרי חלקם דכאו"א המו"ל דינים ומנהגים הנוגעים למעשה. ולסיום הקדמתו — דוקא לעת"ל יתוספו ג' ערי מקלט. וידוע כ' האריז"ל בזה — תיקון אחים הראשונים קין והבל, ועד לשלימות — מאהבה לאחדות.
אזכיר עה"צ.
[quotation](ממכתב כ"ג תמוז, תשמ"ז)[/quotation]
[new\_section]
... במענה לשאלותיו:
גזיזת השערות — יש לדחותה לאחר "בין המצרים". מה שנהגו להדר כו'[ftnref\_1855\_\*] — מיום מלאת ג"ש. . .
[quotation](ממכתב ד' תמוז תשכ"ז)[/quotation]

## 298

... שאלה השני'[ftnref\_1856\_\*] א) כנראה חושב שרז"ל לא העריכו כראוי את גודל ריב"ז. ואינו. ודי לצטט מב'­ג' מקומות.
1) בידיעות התורה ומדעים: (מס' סופרים פט"ז) אם יהיו כל השמים יריעות וכל האילנות קולמוסין וכל הימים דיו, אין כדאי לכתוב החכמה שלמד מרבותיו. 2) במילי דעלמא תהלוכות המדינה: (סוף סוטה) משמת ריב"ז בטל זיו החכמה (בחכמה דעלמא, מהרש"א). 3) בתור מנהיג ישראל: (ספרי ס"פ ברכה) משה הי' במצרים מ' שנה במדין מ' שנה ופרנס את ישראל מ' שנה. ריב"ז עסק בפרקמטיא מ' שנה, שמש חכמים מ' שנה ופרנס את ישראל (הי' נשיא) מ' שנה.
ב) בהערכת המאורעות שבזמן החורבן, המצב הפוליטי אז, מצב רוח הרומאים, אם היתה תקוה לשלום וכו' — איך אפשר לנו, שאנו רחוקים מאז יותר מ­1800 שנה וכמעט שאין תעודות בר סמכא מלבד התלמוד מזמן ההוא, לחוו"ד שלא בהסכם אפילו בפרט קל עם חוו"ד אנשים שחיו בדור ההוא או ב­ שאחריו. — זהו גם אם הי' כתוב בס' דה"י פשוט. עתה שהערכה זו נכנסה בתלמוד, היינו שע"פ השגחה מלמעלה נעשתה חלק מתורה שבעל פה שקדושתה היא כקדושת תושבע"פ, אין שייך בזה שקו"ט אם אמת הדבר, אלא רק השתדלות בהבנת הענין והסברתו, שגם אנחנו נבין אותו.
ג) ראי' מכרעת שריב"ז לא הביט על גז"ד החורבן כענין שאין לדחותו, אלא שהי' משתדל גם אז להציל את ביהמ"ק מהחורבן, הוא זה שטרם הלכו לאספסינוס השתדל [אצל] מנהיגי ירושלים אז שיעשו שלום עם הרומאים. ורק כשלא הצליח בזה הלך לאספסינוס, להשתדל בדברי ריב"ז "אפשר דהוי הצלה פורתא". . .
[quotation](ממכתב שלהי אלול, תש"ח)[/quotation]

**English:**

## 297

### Bein HaMetzarim

And establish the *chazakah* — regarding the matter and the *shakla v'tarya* there in the *halachos* of *Zeman HaMizman* and the *halachos* of *Beis HaBechirah* (and *Ohr Torah*, *Parshas Pinchas*).

And this is the essential *din* and *minhag* during *Bein HaMetzarim*: not only to nullify them, but to transform them into gladness and joy and *moadim tovim* ("And they loved truth and peace" — as is also written in his introduction to his sefer, which was printed in *Torah Temimah*, attached here is 10 dollars).

And fortunate is the portion of each and every publisher of *dinim* and *minhagim* that are relevant for practical application. And in the conclusion of his introduction — specifically for the time to come, three cities of refuge will be added. And the *kav* of the Arizal is known regarding this — a *tikkun* for the first brothers, Kayin and Hevel, and reaching perfection — through love and unity.

I will mention this at the *tziyon*.
(From a letter, 23 Tammuz, 5747)

... In response to your questions:
Cutting hair — it should be postponed until after *Bein HaMetzarim*. Regarding what is practiced to be stringent, etc. — from the day the three years are completed...
(From a letter, 4 Tammuz, 5727)

## 298

... Regarding the second question: a) You apparently think that our Sages did not properly appreciate the greatness of Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai. This is not so. It suffices to cite two or three places:
1) Regarding his knowledge of Torah and the sciences: (*Maseches Sofrim*, ch. 16) If all the heavens were parchment, all the trees were quills, and all the seas were ink, it would not be sufficient to record the wisdom he learned from his teachers. 2) Regarding worldly matters and the conduct of the state: (end of *Sotah*) When Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai died, the luster of wisdom ceased (referring to worldly wisdom, *Maharsha*). 3) As a leader of Israel: (*Sifri*, end of *Parshas Berachah*) Moshe was in Egypt for 40 years, in Midian for 40 years, and sustained Israel for 40 years. Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai engaged in business for 40 years, served the Sages for 40 years, and sustained Israel (he was *Nasi*) for 40 years.

b) Regarding the assessment of the events during the time of the *Churban*, the political situation then, the mood of the Romans, whether there was hope for peace, etc. — how is it possible for us, who are removed from that time by more than 1,800 years and have almost no reliable documentation other than the Talmud from that era, to offer an opinion that does not agree, even in a minor detail, with the opinion of people who lived in that generation or the one that followed? This would be true even if it were written in a simple history book. Now that this assessment has entered the Talmud — meaning that through *Hashgachah* from above it became part of the *Torah Shebe'al Peh*, whose holiness is the holiness of *Torah Shebe'al Peh* — there is no room for *shakla v'tarya* as to whether it is true; rather, the only task is to strive to understand the matter and explain it, so that we, too, may understand it.

c) A decisive proof that Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai did not view the decree of the *Churban* as something that could not be averted, but rather that he was striving even then to save the *Beis HaMikdash* from destruction, is the fact that before he went to Vespasian, he strove [with] the leaders of Jerusalem at that time to make peace with the Romans. And only when he did not succeed in this did he go to Vespasian, to strive with the words of Rabban Yochanan ben Zakkai, "perhaps there will be a small salvation"...
(From a letter, end of Elul, 5708)

## ימי הקיץ

_Section: ימי הקיץ_

**Hebrew:**

## 299

### ימי הקיץ

ב"ה, עש"ק[ftnref\_1857\_1] מה[ftnref\_1858\_2] טובו אוהליך יעקב משכנותיך ישראל. . כגנות עלי נהר[ftnref\_1859\_3],
חדש הגאולה, ה'תשמ"ח, שנת הקהל.
ברוקלין, נ.י.
ל"גן ישראל[ftnref\_1860\_4]"
ה' עליהם יחיו
שלום וברכה!
תהי פתיחת ה"גן ישראל" וכל משך שהותם בו בהצלחה בכל,
ובפרט שהשנה שנת הקהל[ftnref\_1861\_5] את האנשים והנשים והטף גו' לעשות את כל דברי התורה הזאת,
ובגנים[ftnref\_1862\_6] בפרט — הדגשת תורה תפלה צדקה,
והשם עוזר[ftnref\_1863\_7],
ועי"ז גם ממהרים קיום היעוד: ולציון[ftnref\_1864\_8] יאמר גו' והוא יכוננה עליון,
בגאולה[ftnref\_1865\_9] האמתית והשלימה ע"י משיח צדקנו.
בברכת שבת טוב ולהצלחה
[new\_section]

## 300

ב"ה, שלישי[ftnref\_1866\_1] בתמוז[ftnref\_1867\_2][ftnref\_1868\_3],
פרשת ויאמר ה' אני חלקך ונחלתך[ftnref\_1869\_4], חדש הגאולה,
ה'תשמ"ט, ברוקלין, נ.י.
ל"גן ישראל"
ה' עליהם יחיו
שלום וברכה!
תהי פתיחת ה"גן ישראל" וכל משך שהותם בו בהצלחה רבה,
ובשנה תמימה[ftnref\_1870\_5] זו, ובפרט שנמצאים אנו בשנת הארבעים דהסתלקות­הילולא דבעל הגאולה שעל זה נאמר: נתן ה' לכם[ftnref\_1871\_6] לב לדעת ועינים לראות ואזנים לשמוע,
יוסיף כאו"א מכם ובשמחה רבה[ftnref\_1872\_7] בחינוך עצמו ובחינוך הסביבה בתורה (שהיא מורשה[ftnref\_1873\_8] קהלת יעקב) ומצותי',
וימשיכו בכ"ז במשך כל השנה ובהוספה,
וכ"ז יקדים ויזרז קיום התפלה: תבוא[ftnref\_1874\_9] לפניך תפלתי הטה אזנך לרנתי.
בגאולה האמתית והשלימה ע"י משיח צדקנו.
בברכת הצלחה בכל האמור
ובברכת חג הגאולה
[quotation] [/quotation]
  
 [new\_section]

**English:**

## 299

### Summer Days

B"H, Erev Shabbos Kodesh, Parshas Mah Tovu Ohalecha Yaakov Mishkenosecha Yisrael... Kigeinos Alei Nahar,
Month of Geulah, 5748, Year of Hakhel.
Brooklyn, N.Y.

To "Gan Yisrael",
May Hashem grant them life,
Peace and Blessing!

May the opening of "Gan Yisrael" and the entire duration of your stay there be successful in all respects, especially since this year is the year of Hakhel—"Assemble the men, the women, and the children... to perform all the words of this Torah."

In the gardens (ganim) in particular—emphasizing Torah, tefillah, and tzedakah. And Hashem is the Helper. Through this, you also hasten the fulfillment of the promise: "And to Tzion it shall be said... and He, the Most High, shall establish her," with the true and complete Geulah through Moshiach Tzidkeinu.

With blessings for a good Shabbos and for success.

---

## 300

B"H, Tuesday of Tammuz,
Parshas "Hashem said, I am your portion and your inheritance," Month of Geulah,
5749, Brooklyn, N.Y.

To "Gan Yisrael,"
May Hashem grant them life,
Peace and Blessing!

May the opening of "Gan Yisrael" and the entire duration of your stay there be with great success. In this complete year, and especially as we are in the fortieth year since the histalkus-hillula of the Baal HaGeulah—regarding which it is said: "Hashem has given you a heart to know, and eyes to see, and ears to hear"—may each one of you increase, with great joy, in your own education and in the education of your surroundings in Torah (which is the "heritage of the congregation of Yaakov") and its mitzvos.

May you continue in all this throughout the entire year, with added strength. All of this will hasten and accelerate the fulfillment of the prayer: "May my prayer come before You; incline Your ear to my cry," with the true and complete Geulah through Moshiach Tzidkeinu.

With blessings for success in all that is mentioned, and with blessings for the Chag HaGeulah.

---

## אופנים

_Section: אופנים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="23"] (אופנים ושרפים, חילוקם).

**English:**

## Ofanim and Seraphim, their distinction.

The distinction between Ofanim and Seraphim is as follows:

The Ofanim are associated with the attribute of *Malchus*, as it is written, "And I looked, and behold, four wheels (*Ofanim*) beside the *Keruvim*." This reflects the aspect of *bittul* that comes from the realization of *Malchus*—the recognition that "there is nothing else besides Him," which is the level of *bittul* that characterizes the *Olam HaAsiyah*.

The Seraphim, by contrast, are associated with the attribute of *Gevurah* and *Eish* (fire), as it is written, "Seraphim stood above Him." Their *avodah* is one of *ratzo*—a burning, intense yearning to be consumed within the light of the *Ein Sof*.

Thus, the difference between them is the difference between the *bittul* of *Malchus*—which is a state of standing in place and acknowledging the sovereignty of Hashem—and the *bittul* of *Gevurah*, which is a state of active, fiery movement toward the Source.

---

## אייר

_Section: אייר_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="56"] (אייר וניסן — עשה טוב וסור מרע).

**English:**

## Iyar and Nissan — Doing Good and Turning Away from Evil

The month of Iyar follows the month of Nissan. In the order of the avodah of the soul, this corresponds to the sequence of "turn away from evil and do good" (Tehillim 34:15).

Nissan is the time of "turn away from evil"—the process of *iskafya* (subjugation) and *is'hapcha* (transformation) of the *sitra achra*. This is the essence of the exodus from Mitzrayim, which is the "turning away" from the constraints and limitations of the *kelipah*.

Iyar, however, is the time of "do good." It is the period of *sefirat ha'omer*, which is a process of refinement and purification of the seven *middos*—the "doing" of good through the rectification of one's character traits and the elevation of the *nefesh habahamit*.

Thus, the transition from Nissan to Iyar represents the movement from the initial liberation—the departure from the negative—to the constructive building of one's *avodah*, where the *neshamah* actively engages in the refinement of the world, transforming the "good" that is hidden within the material into a dwelling place for Hashem.

## ברכת הגומל

_Section: ברכת הגומל_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="138"].

**English:**

## Birchas HaGomel

 The Gemara states that one who has been saved from four situations must recite Birchas HaGomel: those who have traveled across the sea, those who have traveled through the desert, one who has recovered from an illness, and one who has been released from prison. 

Seemingly, we must understand the common denominator between these four. While it is true that all four involve a state of danger from which one has been rescued, the nature of the danger differs significantly. Furthermore, the Gemara derives these four from the verse in Tehillim, "He sent His word and healed them, and delivered them from their destructions," which implies that the common thread is the concept of "healing" and "deliverance."

But in truth, the matter can be understood by looking at the deeper significance of these four categories within the context of *avodah*. The four categories represent the four primary obstacles that a person encounters in their *avodah* to Hashem:

The "sea" represents the turbulence of the world—the *he'elem v'hester* (concealment) that threatens to overwhelm the *neshamah* as it descends into the body. The "desert" represents the desolate places where there is no *yishuv* (settlement) of *kedushah*, where one is left to navigate without the support of a *kehillah* or the light of Torah. "Illness" refers to the weakening of the *kochos* of the soul, where the *yetzer hara* gains strength through the person’s own spiritual lethargy. And "prison" refers to the state of being constrained by the *gashmius* of the world, where the *neshamah* is unable to express its innate *bitul* and *dveikus* to Hashem.

When a person overcomes these challenges—whether through their own *avodah* or through the *chesed* of Hashem—they are essentially experiencing a "redemption" from the limitations of their own nature. Birchas HaGomel is the acknowledgment that this rescue is not merely a physical survival, but a spiritual rebirth. It is the declaration that the *Ribono shel Olam* has bestowed "good" upon one who is undeserving, recognizing that even when one is trapped in the "sea" or the "desert" of their own making, the *Ohr Ein Sof* remains accessible, waiting to deliver them from their "destructions."

---

## כסלו, י&quot;ט כסלו

_Section: כסלו, י&quot;ט כסלו_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="144"] .

**English:**

## Kislev, 19 Kislev

 The concept of 19 Kislev—the "Rosh Hashanah of Chassidus"—is fundamentally connected to the revelation of the inner dimension of Torah, which is the "soul of Torah." As is known, the revelation of the inner dimension of Torah is the preparation for the coming of Moshiach, as it is written, "When your springs spread outward" (*Mishlei 5:16*).

The connection between the revelation of Chassidus and the redemption is not merely a chronological sequence, but an essential one. The *avodah* of the Alter Rebbe, which culminated in his liberation on 19 Kislev, brought about a new light in the world—a light that enables every Yid, regardless of their level, to connect to the deepest levels of Elokus. This is the essence of the "spreading of the springs": that the deepest, most concealed aspects of the Torah and of Hashem’s essence are brought down into the practical reality of our daily lives, transforming the world into a dwelling place for Him.

Therefore, the celebration of 19 Kislev is not merely a commemoration of a historical event, but an ongoing *avodah*. It is a call to action for every Chossid to internalize the teachings of Chassidus and to live with the consciousness that we are standing on the threshold of the true and complete Geulah. Through our *avodah* of *bitul* and *dveikus*, and through the dissemination of these teachings to others, we hasten the day when the knowledge of Hashem will cover the earth as the waters cover the sea.

---

## סיון

_Section: סיון_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="60"] (במה שא"א תחנון עד י"ב סיון).

**English:**

## Regarding the custom not to say Tachanun until the 12th of Sivan

In the Shulchan Aruch it is ruled that Tachanun is not recited from Rosh Chodesh Sivan until the 12th of Sivan. The reason for this is that these days are associated with the days of the dedication of the Mizbeiach by the Nesi'im, as the 12th of Sivan is the day that concludes the period during which one can bring the Korban Pesach Sheini and the days of the dedication of the Mizbeiach.

Seemingly, however, we must understand: Why is the custom to refrain from saying Tachanun specifically until the 12th of Sivan? If the reason is the dedication of the Mizbeiach, which lasted for twelve days, then the days of the dedication were the first twelve days of Nissan. Why, then, is this custom observed in Sivan?

Furthermore, even if we say that the days of Sivan are connected to the dedication of the Mizbeiach, the dedication itself took place in Nissan. What is the connection to Sivan?

And with this we can understand:

The dedication of the Mizbeiach by the Nesi'im represents the concept of the "dedication" of the world—that the material world (the Mizbeiach, which is made of earth) becomes a dwelling place for the Shechinah. This is the ultimate purpose of the giving of the Torah, which occurred in Sivan: "The lower worlds were elevated to the higher worlds".

The twelve days of the dedication of the Mizbeiach correspond to the twelve tribes of Yisrael. Through the service of the twelve tribes, the entire world is elevated and sanctified. This service is completed and perfected through the giving of the Torah, which unites all the tribes into one entity. Therefore, the days of Sivan—the days of the giving of the Torah—are the time when the "dedication" of the world is fully realized.

Thus, the days from Rosh Chodesh Sivan until the 12th of Sivan are days of "dedication." Just as the days of the dedication of the Mizbeiach were days of joy and feasting, so too are these days in Sivan, and therefore we do not recite Tachanun.

---

## Rosh Chodesh Sivan

..
See also Shavuos, the Festival of Weeks.

---


## י&quot;ב סיון

_Section: י&quot;ב סיון_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="221"].

**English:**

## 12 Sivan

 It is known that the festival of Shavuos is followed by the days of *tashlumin* (make-up days) until the 12th of Sivan. This is because the *korban chagigah* (festival offering) could be brought during these days, as the holiness of the Yom Tov extends throughout this period.

In the context of *avodah*, this teaches us that even after the actual days of Yom Tov have passed, the *hisorerus* (awakening) and the *hispaalus* (emotional arousal) experienced during the Yom Tov—specifically the *matan Torah* (giving of the Torah) that occurred on Shavuos—must continue to permeate one’s *avodah* throughout the following days.

This is the deeper meaning of the 12th of Sivan: it is the final day of the *tashlumin* period, representing the conclusion of the "extension" of the Yom Tov. The lesson is that the *bitul* and the *dveikus* to Hashem that were achieved through the *matan Torah* on Shavuos must be integrated into one’s daily life, even when one is no longer in the immediate atmosphere of the Yom Tov. This is the essence of *avodah*—to take the *taanug* and the *kedushah* of the *neshamah* and bring them down into the reality of the world, ensuring that the *hisrashmus* (impression) of Shavuos remains a constant force in one’s *avodah* of *tefillah* and *limud haTorah* throughout the entire year.

---


## כ&quot;ה סיון

_Section: כ&quot;ה סיון_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="274"].

**English:**

## 28 Sivan

 The concept of the *geulah* is that it is not merely a change in the state of the world, but rather the revelation of the true reality of the world as it exists in its source. As the *Gemara* states, "The world will exist for six thousand years," and the *geulah* is the time when the purpose of this existence is realized.

On the face of it, one might ask: if the *geulah* is the ultimate purpose, why must it be preceded by the long and arduous process of *galus*? Why could the world not have been created in a state of *geulah* from the outset?

But in truth, this is the very essence of our *avodah*. Through the *avodah* of *birur*—refining the sparks of *Elokus* hidden within the material world—we transform the *galus* itself into a vessel for the *geulah*. It is not that the *geulah* arrives to replace the world; rather, the *geulah* is the result of the world itself being elevated to its state of perfection.

This is the meaning of the *maamar* of the *Chazal* regarding the *geulah*: "Everything is ready." The *avodah* of *anash* throughout the generations has already completed the refinement of the world. Therefore, the *geulah* is not a future event that we are waiting for in a passive sense; it is a reality that is already present, awaiting only the final *hisorerus* to be revealed in a manifest way.

And with this we can understand the emphasis on *mesiras nefesh* in our current time. Because we are standing at the very threshold of the *geulah*, every act of *avodah*—every *mitzvah* performed with *kavanah* and *bitul*—carries the weight of the final refinement. We are not merely preparing for the *geulah*; we are actively bringing it into existence through our *avodah*.

---


## כ&quot;ח סיון

_Section: כ&quot;ח סיון_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="274"].

**English:**

## 28 Sivan



**1.** The connection between the date of 28 Sivan and the theme of *hisgalus* (revelation) is well known. This is the day the Rebbe and the Rebbetzin arrived in the United States in 5701, an event that marked the beginning of the *hisgalus* of the Rebbe’s leadership and the subsequent dissemination of the wellsprings of Chassidus to the furthest reaches of the world.

**2.** In the *maamar* titled *Basi L’Gani* 5711, the Rebbe explains that the purpose of the *yiridah* (descent) of the *neshamah* into this world is to transform the world into a *dirah b’tachtonim*—a dwelling place for the *Ribono shel Olam* in the lowest realms. This is achieved specifically through the *avodah* of *is’hapcha* (transforming darkness into light), which is the essence of the *avodah* of the seventh generation.

**3.** The *hisgalus* of the Rebbe is not merely a historical event, but a continuous process of *avodah*. Just as the *hisgalus* of the *Ohr Ein Sof* in the world requires the *keli* (vessel) of our *avodah*, so too the *hisgalus* of the *Nasi* requires the *hiskashrus* and *avodah* of *anash*. When a *chossid* engages in *avodah* with *bitul* and *mesiras nefesh*, he creates the *keli* for the *hisgalus* of the Rebbe’s *hashpa'ah* (influence) in his own life and in the world at large.

**4.** Therefore, the *avodah* of 28 Sivan is to internalize this *hisgalus*. It is not enough to commemorate the date; one must translate the *hisgalus* into a practical *avodah* of *hafatzas hamaayanos*. By strengthening our *hiskashrus* to the Rebbe and committing ourselves to the *avodah* of the seventh generation, we hasten the ultimate *hisgalus* of *Moshiach Tzidkeinu*, when the *Ohr Ein Sof* will be revealed in this world in a complete and manifest way.

---

## פדיון שבויים

_Section: פדיון שבויים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="289"].

**English:**

## Pidyon Shvuyim

 The concept of *pidyon shvuyim*—the redemption of captives—is a mitzvah that encompasses the entire essence of the Jewish soul. As our Sages state, "There is no greater mitzvah than *pidyon shvuyim*." This is not merely a quantitative statement regarding the importance of this mitzvah relative to others, but rather an expression of its qualitative nature: it touches upon the very core of *Klal Yisrael*.

Seemingly, one might ask: why is this mitzvah singled out as being so supreme? After all, the Torah contains many mitzvos that involve *mesiras nefesh* and profound *avodah*. Yet, when we examine the inner dimension of *pidyon shvuyim*, we understand that it is the ultimate expression of the bond between Hashem and the Jewish people. A *Yid* is never truly "captured" or separated from the source of holiness; even in the lowest depths of *galus*, the *neshamah* remains connected to the *Ein Sof*. The act of *pidyon shvuyim* is the external manifestation of this internal reality—it is the process of revealing that even that which appears to be in the domain of the "other" actually belongs to Hashem.

This is the deeper meaning behind the *avodah* of every *shaliach* and every *Chossid*. We are tasked with the "redemption" of the sparks of *Elokus* scattered throughout the world. By engaging in this *avodah* with *bitul* and *mesiras nefesh*, we not only liberate the captive, but we elevate the entire *seder hishtalshelus*, bringing the world closer to the ultimate *geulah*—the final *pidyon* of the entire Jewish people from the final *galus*.

---

_Section: פורים_

## פטרבורג (עיר)

_Section: פטרבורג (עיר)_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="142"].

**English:**

## Petersburg (City)

 The city of Petersburg is known as a place of *geulah* and liberation, for it was there that the Alter Rebbe was released from his imprisonment, and later, the Rayatz was also released from his imprisonment in that city. 

This is consistent with the general principle that the *avodah* of a Chossid is to transform the "Petersburg" of the world—the places of darkness and concealment—into a place of *geulah*. Through the *avodah* of *mesiras nefesh* and *bitul*, one reveals that even in the most distant and concealed places, there is nothing but the light of Hashem. This is the essence of the *avodah* of *galus*: to transform the *galus* itself into *geulah*, until the ultimate *geulah* with Moshiach Tzidkeinu, when the light of Hashem will be revealed in all its glory, and the entire world will become a dwelling place for Him.

---

## פשרות

_Section: פשרות_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="147"] (שלילת פשרות בעניני התורה).

**English:**

## The Rejection of Compromise in Matters of Torah

The concept of compromise—*pesharah*—is a fundamental principle in the realm of interpersonal relations and civil law. The Gemara teaches that "a compromise is a mitzvah," as it fosters peace and harmony between parties. However, this applies exclusively to matters of civil litigation, where the objective is to reach an equitable settlement between two individuals.

When it comes to matters of Torah and mitzvos, however, the very notion of compromise is fundamentally misplaced. Torah is the expression of the Will of Hashem, which is absolute and immutable. One cannot "compromise" on the truth of Hashem or the integrity of His mitzvos, for doing so would imply that the Torah is subject to human negotiation or situational adjustment. In the realm of holiness, there is no middle ground, for any deviation from the precise path of the Torah is not a "compromise"—it is a departure from the truth itself.

Thus, while in the world of mundane affairs we seek to bridge gaps and find common ground, in the service of Hashem, our *avodah* is to stand firm in our commitment to the Torah’s demands, without dilution or concession. True peace—*shalom*—is not achieved by compromising the truth, but by elevating the world to the standard of the Torah, thereby revealing that the entire world is, in essence, a vessel for the light of Hashem.

---

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="226"] (צדקה תרומם גוי). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="230"] (שליחות מצוה לצדקה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="232"] (חיזוק מוסדות התורה ליובאוויטש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="234"] (תמיכה במוסדות חי­נוך). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="269"] (כנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="286"] (כנ"ל).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls33" goterm="כפר חב"ד"].

**English:**

## Tzedakah

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="226"] (Tzedakah exalts a nation). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="230"] (The shlichus of a mitzvah for the sake of tzedakah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="232"] (Strengthening the Torah institutions of Lubavitch). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="234"] (Support for educational institutions). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="269"] (Same as above). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="286"] (Same as above).
See also [mafteach_goterm file="ls33" goterm="כפר חב"ד"] (Kfar Chabad).

## ציצית

_Section: ציצית_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="95"] (בחילוק השיטות דלבישת הט"ק על הבגדים או תחת הבגדים. אם הציציות צ"ל בחוץ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="96"] (הבגד עצמו אין בו קדו­שה. ההסתכלות בציצית). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="99"] (ציצית — ע"ש הפתילים או ע"ש הראי'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="100"] (בגדר מצות ראי' וזכירה בציצית). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="101"] (שקולה כנגד כל המצות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="102"] (לבישת ט"ק ב­לילה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="104"] (מצות ציצית של הקב"ה — והביאנו לשלום מארבע כנפות הארץ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="152"] (ק"ש ציצית ותפילין קדימתן זל"ז). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="153"] (שקולה כנגד כה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="273"] (ציצית בלילה ושינה בט"ק. ט"ק שומר ומגין על הנלבש. באיסור הטלת חוטי צמר בטלית של פשתן).

**English:**

## Tzitzis

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="95"] (Regarding the difference in opinions concerning wearing the tallis katan over one's garments or beneath them; whether the tzitzis must be visible on the outside.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="96"] (The garment itself possesses no inherent holiness; the act of looking at the tzitzis.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="99"] (Tzitzis — whether the name derives from the threads or from the act of looking.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="100"] (Defining the mitzvah of seeing and remembering through tzitzis.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="101"] (Equivalent to all the mitzvos.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="102"] (Wearing a tallis katan at night.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="104"] (The mitzvah of tzitzis of the Ribono shel Olam — "And bring us in peace from the four corners of the earth.") [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="152"] (The order of priority between Krias Shema, tzitzis, and tefillin.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="153"] (Equivalent to the entire Torah.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="273"] (Tzitzis at night and sleeping in a tallis katan; the tallis katan as a source of protection and guarding for the one who wears it; the prohibition of attaching wool threads to a linen tallis.)

Regarding the dispute as to whether only the first verse is required by Torah law or more than that). (Its effect upon the entire day).

## שבועות, חה&quot;ש

_Section: שבועות, חה&quot;ש_

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="216"] (ה­הכנה דויחן שם ישראל כאיש אחד כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="217"] (ההכנה דימי הגבלה. הכל מודים ב­עצרת דבעינן נמי לכם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="221"] (סיום ימי התשלומין בי"ב סיון או בי"ג בו. ויחן שם ישראל כאיש אחד כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="223"] (כנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="224"] (שלשת ימי הגבלה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="226"] (ויחן שם ישראל כאיש אחד כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="228"] (כנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="229"] (שלשת ימי הגבלה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="231"] (במנהג אכילת מאכלי חלב בחה"ש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="231"] (קיום מצות שחיטה לפני מ"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="33"]-[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="232"] (שייכות מיוחדת לנשי ישראל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="234"] (שייכותו לקטנים, בנינו עורבים אותנו). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="262"] (נוסח המברקים לקראת חה"ש).

**English:**

## Shavuos, Chag HaShavuos

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="9"] (The level of yechidah, absolute dveikus in Hashem). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="18"] (Regarding the Haftorah of Maaseh Merkavah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="41"] (The revelation of the essential hiskashrus between Yisrael and the Ribono shel Olam). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="57"] (The Shtei HaLechem, which is chametz). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="216"] (The preparation of "and Yisrael camped there as one man, etc."). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="217"] (The preparation of the Yemei Hagbalah. Everyone agrees that on Atzeres we also require "for you"). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="218"] (Its special connection to the year of Hakhel. "And Yisrael camped there as one man, etc."). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="221"] (The conclusion of the days of tashlumin on the 12th of Sivan or the 13th. "And Yisrael camped there as one man, etc."). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="223"] (As above). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="224"] (The three Yemei Hagbalah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="226"] ("And Yisrael camped there as one man, etc."). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="228"] (As above). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="229"] (The three Yemei Hagbalah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="231"] (Regarding the custom of eating dairy foods on Chag HaShavuos). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="231"] (The fulfillment of the mitzvah of shechitah before Matan Torah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="33"]-[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="232"] (Special connection to the women of Yisrael). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="234"] (Its connection to children, "our children are our guarantors"). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="262"] (The text of telegrams for Chag HaShavuos).

## שבטים

_Section: שבטים_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="129"] (בדעת הרמב"ם דשבט אינו כלה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="195"] (ההבדל בין "שבט" ל"מטה" בעבודת האדם).

**English:**

## שבטים

 Regarding the opinion of the Rambam that a *shevet* (tribe) is never nullified. The difference between *shevet* and *mateh* in the *avodah* of man.

## שבת

_Section: שבת_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="27"] (נצטוו עלי' במרה או באלוש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="62"] (אם יש מצות שמחה בשבת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="143"] (הארת קדו­שתו ית' ע"ד שעומדים לפני המלך וע"כ אסור במלאכה. דרגות: חוה"מ, יו"ט, שבת, יוהכ"פ). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="159"] (דחיית תענית ב­שבת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="160"] (אין עצב בה. אכילת שבת עונג הנשמה. לפעמים מגשם. פרש שבת­כם לא קאמר).
ע"ע [mafteach\_goterm file="ls33" displayterm="גר" goterm="גרות"].

**English:**

## Shabbos

 (Commanded at Marah or at Alush). (Whether there is a mitzvah of simcha on Shabbos). (The illumination of His holiness, may He be blessed, in the manner of standing before the King, and therefore work is forbidden. Levels: Chol HaMoed, Yom Tov, Shabbos, Yom Kippur). (Postponing a fast on Shabbos). (There is no sadness in it. Eating on Shabbos is an oneg for the neshamah. Sometimes [it is] from the physical. It does not say "spread your Shabbos").
See also (Ger/Conversion).

## שואה

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="253"] (הי' בהסכמת אנשי המדע ומייסדי שיטות חקירה ומוסר כו' מהעם ההוא). ## Shoah

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="253"] (It occurred with the consent of the scientists and the founders of systems of inquiry and ethics, etc., from that nation.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="257"] (Differences between the Shoah and other decrees and persecutions. With the consent of the philosophers and scientists. The question regarding the possibility of the Shoah.) [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="259"] (One must not be content with merely remembering the Shoah, but must also act in the opposite direction—that "the more they were oppressed, the more they multiplied and spread.") [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="260"] (The question regarding the possibility of the Shoah.)

## שלמה, רש&quot;י (כללים בפירושו)

_Section: שלמה, רש&quot;י (כללים בפירושו)_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="2"] (אין דרכו לכתוב סתם טעמי המצות וההלכות). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="13"] (ההדגשה ד"יינה של תורה" הוא בפירושו עה"ת, משא"כ בפי' הש"ס). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="73"] (בפירושו עה"ת דרכו לשנות מלשון חז"ל בהתאם לפשש"מ. הבאת שם בעל המאמר בפירושו הוא לרמוז על תוס' הסברה כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="76"] (כללים כשמביא את התרגום. ה"כללים בדברי רש"י" הנדפס בכמה חומשים צ"ע מי חיברם). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="106"] (שינויי גירסאות ברש"י לפעמים הגירסא הבלתי מובנת היא הנכונה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="109"] (בלשון רש"י: והמדרש אומר כו' אם כוונתו לש­לול הפירוש הפשוט). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="131"] (ענינו לפרש פשש"מ ולא להעתיק ל' המדרש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="149"] (במשלים ושירות שבתורה הוא מפרש גם ע"ד הדרש. כללים בהבאתו התרגום). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="171"] (אם לפעמים מפרש במקום א' לדיעה א' ובמקום שני לפי דיעה אחרת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="177"] (כש­מביא ב' פירושים ה"ז מפני שבכ"א מהם יש קושי שאינו בהשני כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="178"] (מביא אגדה רק אם צריכים לדברי אגדה. ל­פעמים תיבות שהם מתוך פירושו נדפסו בט"ס בתור ד"ה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="181"] (בלשון רש"י: ו­מדרשו באגדה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="208"] (מפרש טעמי ה­מצות רק כשנוגע להבנת הפשט).

**English:**

## Shlomo, Rashi (Principles in his Commentary)

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="2"] It is not his custom to write the reasons for the mitzvos and the halachos without cause.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="13"] The emphasis on "the wine of Torah" is in his commentary on the Torah, as opposed to his commentary on the Shas.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="73"] In his commentary on the Torah, it is his custom to alter the language of Chazal in accordance with the *peshuto shel mikra*. Citing the name of the author of the statement in his commentary is intended to hint at an additional explanation, etc.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="76"] Principles regarding when he cites the Targum. The "Principles in the Words of Rashi" printed in many Chumashim require study as to who authored them.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="106"] Regarding textual variants in Rashi: sometimes the version that seems incomprehensible is the correct one.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="109"] Regarding the language of Rashi: "And the Midrash says, etc." — if his intent is to negate the simple interpretation.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="131"] His objective is to explain the *peshuto shel mikra* and not to copy the language of the Midrash.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="149"] In the parables and songs in the Torah, he also interprets according to the *drash*. Principles regarding his citation of the Targum.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="171"] If he sometimes interprets in one place according to one opinion and in a second place according to a different opinion.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="177"] When he brings two interpretations, it is because each of them contains a difficulty that is not present in the other, etc.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="178"] He brings Aggadah only if the words of Aggadah are necessary. Sometimes words that are part of his own commentary have been printed by mistake as a *dibbur hamaschil*.

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="181"] Regarding the language of Rashi: "And its Midrash in Aggadah."

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="208"] He explains the reasons for the mitzvos only when it pertains to understanding the *peshat*.

---


## שלמי שמחה

_Section: שלמי שמחה_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="66"].

**English:**

## שלמי שמחה



The Gemara states that "one who is happy should bring a *korban* of *shelamim*." This is understood in the context of the *avodah* of a *chossid*: the *korban shelamim* represents the state of *shleimus* (wholeness/perfection) that results from *avodah*. 

Seemingly, one might ask: if a person has already reached a state of *shleimus* and happiness, why is there a need for a *korban*? A *korban* is a means of drawing closer to Hashem, typically necessitated by a lack or a need for elevation. Yet here, the *korban* is brought specifically *because* of the happiness and the *shleimus* that has already been attained.

But in truth, this is precisely the point. The *shleimus* of a *chossid* is not a static destination; it is a dynamic state of *ratzo v'shov*. The very happiness that one experiences—the *taanug* derived from one's *avodah*—must itself be brought as an offering to Hashem. By bringing the *korban shelamim*, the *chossid* acknowledges that his entire state of *shleimus* is not a personal acquisition, but a gift from the Ribono shel Olam. 

And with this we can understand the deeper significance of *simcha* in *avodah*. It is not merely an emotional byproduct; it is the vessel through which the *ohr* of the *mitzvah* is internalized. When the *simcha* is offered as a *korban*, it ceases to be a self-contained feeling and becomes a *dveikus*—a total *bitul* of the *nefesh* to the source of all *taanug*. This is the ultimate *shleimus*: that even in one's highest moments of joy, the *chossid* remains entirely bound to Hashem.

---

## שערות

_Section: שערות_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="263"] (במקור הדין דכיסוי ראש באשה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="264"] (צ"ל כיסוי כל הראש באשה. א"א שקיום מצות ה' דכיסוי ראש יביא לכאב ראש וכיו"ב). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="265"] (חילוק בכיסוי הראש בפאה נכרית או במטפחת. צ"ל מיד בלי עיכוב). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="266"] (תוכחה וצער מהעדר כיסוי הראש באשה). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="268"] (הסכמה לשידוך אפשרית רק כשמוסכם להתנהג ע"פ יסו­די התומ"צ וכיסוי הראש כו').

**English:**

## Hair Covering

[ftnref_ls33_263] Regarding the law of hair covering for a woman.

[ftnref_ls33_264] It is necessary that the entire head of a woman be covered. It is impossible that the fulfillment of Hashem’s mitzvah of hair covering would cause a headache or the like.

[ftnref_ls33_265] A distinction regarding hair covering with a wig versus a headscarf. It must be done immediately, without delay.

[ftnref_ls33_266] Rebuke and distress regarding the lack of hair covering for a woman.

[ftnref_ls33_268] Agreement to a shidduch is possible only when there is an agreement to conduct oneself according to the foundations of Torah and mitzvos, including hair covering, etc.

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="43"] (ב' אופנים ברוממותה: בגד­רי העולם, ולמע' מגדרי העולם. מאן מל­כי רבנן). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="44"] (לימודה בביטול. מאן מלכי רבנן. גדולה מכה"ג הנכנס לפני ולפנים). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="45"] (ביטול בלימוה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="48"] (כנ"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="83"] (ענינה אמת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="118"] (גם דברים שאח"כ נדחו להלכה ואמרו בדותא היא הם דבר ה' כו'). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="145"] (רק התורה קובעת בכל עניני יהדות ואין הדבר מסור לשכל בנ"א). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="224"] (לא בשמים היא). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="230"] (הוספה בלימוד התורה).

**English:**

## Torah

[mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="7"] (Longer than the earth in measure. If it is within the category of measure and boundary). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="17"] (It effects peace and unity in the world. Dovid would connect the Torah from above with the Ribono shel Olam. Learning Torah lishmah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="24"] (It speaks of the supernal realms and alludes to the lower ones). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="33"] (Through Torah, "You are taking Me," the Atzmut of Hashem). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="39"] (It connects Yisrael with the Ribono shel Olam). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="40"] (Learning Torah lishmah. Whoever honors the Torah, his own body is honored, etc.). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="42"] (Its loftiness over the other crowns). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="43"] (Two modes in its loftiness: within the boundaries of the world, and above the boundaries of the world. Who are the kings? The Rabbanan). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="44"] (Learning it with bitul. Who are the kings? The Rabbanan. It is greater than the Kohen Gadol who enters the Holy of Holies). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="45"] (Bitul in learning Torah). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="48"] (As above). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="83"] (Its essence is truth). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="118"] (Even matters that were later rejected in halacha and labeled as "fictions" are the word of Hashem, etc.). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="145"] (Only the Torah determines all matters of Yiddishkeit, and the matter is not left to human intellect). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="224"] (It is not in heaven). [mafteach_gopage file="ls33" gopage="230"] (Increasing in the study of Torah).

## תענית

_Section: תענית_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="156"] (תענית נדחה — תוכנו וגדרו ע"פ הלכה וע"פ פנימיה"ת). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="159"] (ב' ענינים בהד' צומות: יום רצון לה' וגדר אבלות. ביטולם והפיכתם ליו"ט לע"ל). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="160"] (לחיותם ברעב דיוהכ"פ נמשך גם לשאר הצומות. החיות ביום צום מבחי' מדבר שלמע' מדצ"ח). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="163"] (הד' צומות ביטולם והפיכתם ליו"ט לעת"ל).

**English:**

## Ta'anis

 A postponed fast — its content and definition according to Halacha and according to Pnimiyus HaTorah. Two aspects of the four fasts: a day of favor for Hashem and a dimension of mourning. Their nullification and transformation into Yom Tov in the time to come. The vitality during the hunger of Yom Kippur extends also to the other fasts. The vitality on a fast day is from the level of "speaker" (medaber) which is above the levels of inanimate, vegetative, and animate (domem, tzomei'ach, chai). The nullification of the four fasts and their transformation into Yom Tov in the time to come.

---

## Tefillin

 Their essence is that of a testimony and a sign. Regarding the laws of care for the honor of Tefillin. The order of precedence between Krias Shema, Tzitzis, and Tefillin. Connected to the remembrance of the Exodus from Egypt, which is a foundation for many mitzvos.

## במדבר

_Section: במדבר_

**Hebrew:**

[mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="17"] (שייכות הפ' לחה"ש). [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="47"] (כנ"ל).
ר"פ באחד לחודש השני גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="56"].
ברש"י: מתוך חיבתן כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="1"].
א,ב ברש"י ד"ה לגולגלותם: ע"י שקלים כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="3"].
ברמב"ן (בשם רש"י): כמו שאומרים לקוסטינר ארים רישי' דדין: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="2"].
א,ד ואתכם יהיו איש איש למטה גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="203"].
א,מז והלוים למטה אבותם לא התפקדו בתוכם: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="2"].
א,מט אך את מטה לוי לא תפקוד (ורש"י: כדאי הוא כו' ד"א צפה כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="1"].
א,נ ואתה הפקד את הלוים גו' (ורש"י): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="3"].
ב,י דגל מחנה ראובן תימנה: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="10"].
ג,יב ואני הנה לקחתי גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="3"].
ברש"י: וכשחטאו כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="6"].
ג,טו פקוד את בני לוי גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="1"].
ברש"י: משיצא מכלל נפלים כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="2"].
ג,טז עפ"י ה' (ורש"י: הלך משה ועמד כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="4"].
ג,כג משפחות הגרשוני: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="10"].
ג,כט משפחות בני קהת יחנו גו' תימנה (ורש"י: וסמוכין כו' אוי לרשע כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="10"].
ג,לח משה ואהרן ובניו (ורש"י: וסמוכין להם כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="11"].
ג,לט ברש"י שאהרן לא הי' במנין הלוים: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="8"].
ד,ב ברש"י: מנה מהן כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="5"].

## Bamidbar

 (The relevance of the Parsha to the time of the year). (Ibid.).
Beginning of the Parsha: "On the first day of the second month...".
In Rashi: "Because of His love for them...".
1:2 In Rashi, s.v. *Legulgolam*: "By means of shekalim...".
In Ramban (quoting Rashi): "As they say to the executioner, 'Lift the head of this one'...".
1:4 "And with you there shall be a man, each man for his tribe...".
1:47 "But the Levites, according to the tribe of their fathers, were not numbered among them.".
1:49 "However, you shall not number the tribe of Levi" (and Rashi: "He is worthy..." / "Another explanation: He foresaw...".
1:50 "And you shall appoint the Levites..." (and Rashi).
2:10 "The banner of the camp of Reuven shall be to the south.".
3:12 "And I, behold, I have taken...".
In Rashi: "And when they sinned...".
3:15 "Number the children of Levi...".
In Rashi: "Once he has emerged from the category of *nefalim*...".
3:16 "By the word of Hashem" (and Rashi: "Moshe went and stood...".
3:23 "The families of the Gershuni.".
3:29 "The families of the children of Kehas shall encamp... to the south" (and Rashi: "And they are adjacent..." / "Woe to the wicked...".
3:38 "Moshe and Aharon and his sons" (and Rashi: "And adjacent to them...".
3:39 In Rashi: That Aharon was not included in the census of the Levites..
4:2 In Rashi: "Count from among them...".

_Section: בהעלותך_

**Hebrew:**

ר"פ בהעלותך את הנרות גו' (ורש"י: שתהא השלהבת עולה מאלי'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="222"]. [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="269"].

באוה"ח: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="56"].

ט,כג ע"פ ה' ביד משה: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="270"].
י,י וביום שמחתכם גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="62"].

י,לג ברש"י: לתקן להם מקום חניי': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="81"].

יא,ב ויצעק העם אל משה (ורש"י: משל למלך כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="73"].
יא,ד מי יאכילנו בשר (ורש"י: וכי לא הי' להם בשר כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="74"].
יא,ה זכרנו את הדגה גו' את הקשואים גו' (ורש"י: מפני מה המן משתנה כו'): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="71"].
ברש"י: הקשואים הם קוקומברו"ש כו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="75"].
יא,ח והי' טעמו כטעם לשד השמן: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="72"].
יא,כא שש מאות אלף רגלי העם גו': [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="74"].

יג,יז וישלח אותם משה: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="133"].
לתור את ארץ כנען: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="82"].

יג,כה תור הארץ: [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="81"].
יג,כו וילכו ויבואו גו' (ורש"י: מה ביאתן בעצה רעה אף הליכתן בעצה רעה): [mafteach\_gopage file="ls33" gopage="78"].

## Shelach

Beginning of the parsha: "Shelach lecha..." (And Rashi: "According to your own judgment..."): [ftnref_ls33_79]. [ftnref_ls33_133]. [ftnref_ls33_278].
In Rashi: "Because of the slander...": [ftnref_ls33_80].
In Rashi: "And these wicked ones...": [ftnref_ls33_78].
"And they shall scout the land of Canaan": [ftnref_ls33_81].
"One man, one man for each of his father’s tribes...": [ftnref_ls33_203].

"All of them were men" (And Rashi: "And at that time they were righteous"): [ftnref_ls33_78].
"The heads of the Bnei Yisrael" (And Rashi s.v. "All of them"): [ftnref_ls33_80].
13:16 "The men whom Moshe sent": [ftnref_ls33_133].
"And Moshe called... [to Hoshea]..." (And Rashi: "He prayed for him, 'May Yah save you from the counsel of the spies'"): [ftnref_ls33_78].
13:17 "And Moshe sent them": [ftnref_ls33_133].
"To scout the land of Canaan": [ftnref_ls33_82].
13:22 In Rashi: "He prostrated himself on the graves of the forefathers so that they would not... [be part of] their counsel": [ftnref_ls33_80].
13:23 In Rashi: "They intended to bring forth a slander": [ftnref_ls33_80].
13:25 "Scouting the land": [ftnref_ls33_81].
13:26 "And they went and came..." (And Rashi: "Just as their coming was with evil counsel, so too their going was with evil counsel"): [ftnref_ls33_78].
13:28 In Rashi: "And its translation is...": [ftnref_ls33_76].
13:31 "For it is stronger than we" (And Rashi): [ftnref_ls33_82].
13:32 "And they brought forth a slander of the land...": [ftnref_ls33_80].
14:6 "And Yehoshua... from those who scouted the land...": [ftnref_ls33_82].
"Scouting" (*tarim*): [ftnref_ls33_81].
14:7 "The land is very, very good": [ftnref_ls33_92].
14:7-9 "The land is good... do not fear them": [ftnref_ls33_84].
14:8 "If Hashem desires us...": [ftnref_ls33_92].
14:9 "Do not rebel against Hashem": [ftnref_ls33_85].
14:12 "I will strike them... and I will make you into a great nation": [ftnref_ls33_6].
14:27 "The congregation" (And Rashi): [ftnref_ls33_85].
In Rashi s.v. "Which" (and thereafter): [ftnref_ls33_85].

## Korach

Beginning of the Parsha: "And Korach took..." 
Rashi, s.v. Dathan and Aviram: 
16:5, in Ibn Ezra: 
16:6, "Do this: take for yourselves fire-pans..." (and Rashi: "What did he see..."): 
In Rashi: "Here is for you a vessel more beloved than all...": 
16:7, in Rashi: "And he did not see clearly, because his sons did teshuvah": 
16:8, in Ramban: 
16:12, "To call Dathan and Aviram" (and Rashi: "That Moshe was trying to..."): 
16:15, "Do not turn to their offering" (and Rashi: "According to the simple meaning..."): 
16:16-17, "You... each man his fire-pan... and bring them before Hashem": 
16:25, "And Moshe arose and went...": 
16:27, in Rashi, s.v. "And their wives, and their sons, and their little ones": 
16:28, "By this you shall know that Hashem has sent me...": 
16:30, "But if Hashem creates a creation...": 
16:32, "And the earth opened its mouth... and all that belonged to Korach": 
16:33, "And they went down, they and all that they had... and they perished from the midst of the assembly": 
16:35, "And a fire went out... those who offered the incense": 
17:2, "Speak... and lift up the fire-pans...": 
17:3, in Rashi: 
17:5, "In order that no stranger should approach...": 
17:13, in Rashi: "Through him Nadav and Avihu died...": 
17:23, "The staff of Aharon had budded...": 
18:12, "All the best of the oil...": 
18:26, "And you shall set aside from it": 
18:28, "So shall you also set aside...": 
18:29, "Of all its best": 
18:30, "When you have set aside its best from it...": 
18:32, "And you shall not bear sin upon it...":

## Matos

 Regarding the name of the parsha and its connection to the Three Weeks.
At the beginning of the parsha, "Roshei Hamatos" (and Rashi: "He accorded honor, etc., and what did he see, etc."):,.
In Rashi: "It is stated here 'zeh hadavar' and it is stated regarding slaughtering outside [the camp], etc.":.
In Rashi: "To say that a chacham [uses] the language of annulment, etc.":,.
30:3, "Lo yachel dvaro, etc." (and Rashi: "Like 'lo yechallel,' etc."):,.
In Kli Yakar, s.v. "Lo yachel":.
30:4, "B'veis aviha" (and Rashi: "In the domain of her father"):.
31:3, In Rashi: "Even though he heard that his death was dependent on the matter, etc.":.
31:6, In Rashi: "That Pinchas was equal to all of them":.
31:9, "Vayishvu Bnei Yisroel, etc.":.
31:28, "Vaharimosa mech'es laHashem, etc.":.
32:3, In Bachaye:.
32:6, "Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?":.
32:20, "If you do, etc., if you arm yourselves, etc.":.
32:38, "Musavot shem" (and Rashi):.
